title: Morellian
content:

{{Quote|He is the foremost expert in his field; our late companion Major Glidamir once commented that if Barosa were left to die in the wastes of Tatooine, in no time he would build himself a castle of seaweed.|Sisquoc, on the Morellian survival instructor Barosa Warren|<ref name="AIR" />}}
The '''Morellians''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[near-Human]]s native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Morellia/Legends|Morellia]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. They dominated the [[Morellian Commonwealth/Legends|Morellian Commonwealth]], a region patrolled by a [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement body]] known as the [[Morellian Enforcers]]. The Enforcers were known for their choice of weaponry: the [[.48-caliber Enforcer pistol]] was a [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrower]] manufactured by the [[Morellian Weapons Conglomerate/Legends|Morellian Weapons Conglomerate]] that was as strong as a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]]. The Morellians had long lifespans proportionate to [[Human/Legends|Humans]]; at age 110, a Morellian had yet to reach [[Gender/Legends|their]] best [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Nevertheless, by the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the species was nearing [[Death/Legends|extinction]]. The Morellian [[Barosa Warren]] headed the [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]] and posed a threat to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Four of Warren's [[Hybrid/Legends|half-Human]] children served as Rebel [[operative]]s.

==Biology and appearance==
As a [[near-Human]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="NEGWT xxiv-xxv" /> the Morellians looked very similar to [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. Each member of the species had a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] body characterized by two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] with five-fingered hands, a torso, and a head. The hands sported five fingers each, the digits capped by fingernails. The face featured a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] full of [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], a [[Nose/Legends|nose]], two [[Ear/Legends|ears]], and two [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] under a pair of eyebrows. [[Hair/Legends|Hair]] crowned the [[Bone/Legends|skull]]. Similarly, the species was divided into two [[Gender/Legends|sexes]], male and female. Men were able to grow facial [[Hair/Legends|hair]], with at least one Morellian sporting a [[Color/Legends|red]] beard. Members of the species were distinguished from Humans due to their extra-long lifespans; at 110 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old, a Morellian had yet to reach his or her prime, and not until age 160 did a Morellian typically show signs of aging.Despite these differences, Morellians and Humans were capable of [[Reproduction/Legends|mating]] with one another and producing [[Youngling/Legends|offspring]], at least when the male was Morellian and the female Human. Morellian–Human [[Hybrid/Legends|hybrids]] appeared fully Human, with one such specimen sporting a dark complexion and black hair.<ref name="AIR" />

==Society and culture==
[[File:Enforcer-GFTPG-19.jpg|thumb|left|The .48-caliber Enforcer pistol was standard issue to the Morellian Enforcers.]]
The Morellians inhabited the [[Morellian Commonwealth/Legends|Morellian Commonwealth]], a collection of [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] that lay outside the borders of the well-explored [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]<ref name="AIR" /> in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]];<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was [[Morellia/Legends|Morellia]]. Members of the species were able to learn [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="AIR" />

In general, the species lagged behind the rest of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] in technology.<ref name="AIR" /> Nevertheless, the [[Morellian Weapons Conglomerate/Legends|Morellian Weapons Conglomerate]]<ref name="NEGWT xxiv-xxv" />—also known by its initials, MWC<ref name="AIR" />—named itself after the species and created low-tech products, such as a [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrower]] called the [[.48-caliber Enforcer pistol]].<ref name="NEGWT xxiv-xxv" /> Despite its primitive payload,<ref name="GFTPG 19">''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]''</ref> the gun was capable of causing as much damage as a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]], albeit with a recoil so fierce that further shots suffered in accuracy if fired in quick succession.<ref name="A&EG 23">''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]''</ref> Morellians who received specialized training with the sidearm 18 proved their species capable of great strength and skill.<ref name="AIR" />

Although the .48-caliber Enforcer was used by MWC's own corporate security force,<ref name="A&EG 23" /> it was standard issue and the signature weapon of the [[Morellian Enforcers]], a small body tasked with policing the Morellian Commonwealth. The Enforcers wore uniforms that consisted of full-body, [[Leather/Legends|leather]] [[Morellian oilcoat]]s and [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and tough backstrap [[Glove/Legends|gloves]]. The Morellian Enforcers had little access to [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and instead employed [[Slugthrower pistol/Legends|slugthrower pistols]] and [[Slugthrower rifle/Legends|rifles]].<ref name="AIR" />

==History==
Although the Morellians dominated the area of Wild Space known as the Morellian Commonwealth,<ref name="EAOC" /><ref name="AIR" /> by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the species had nearly [[Death/Legends|died]] out. Indeed, in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], the [[Task Force on Alliance Security]]—part of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]—surmised that the Morellian [[Barosa Warren]] was the last of his kind.<ref name="AIR" /> Nevertheless, the Commonwealth was still a recognized region and lay beyond the borders of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="EA 34">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> By the outbreak of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the Morellians were nearly extinct.<ref name="NEGWT xxiv-xxv" />

==Morellians in the galaxy==
{{Quote|Most often he makes use of his Enforcer, a gun that should be part of a Star Destroyer's arsenal rather than a hand weapon. Despite the weapon's obvious weight, he handles it as if it weighs nothing. He can speed draw faster than even Derembus, and that's no simple feat. The recoil on that thing can knock a Gamorrean down, but Warren just squeezes off round after round without effort. If you encounter him and he even flinches, either apologize or find suitable cover. Don't think a landspeeder is enough cover: you should have seen what he did to Maashan's XP-38…|[[Ma'w'shiye]], on Barosa Warren's choice of sidearm|<ref name="AIR" />}}
[[File:Alton_Lochner.png|thumb|right|upright|Alliance operative Alton Lochner was a Morellian–Human hybrid.]]
Barosa Warren was a Morellian survival instructor who was widely regarded as the galaxy's foremost expert on survival in hostile environments. He served as a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] and Morellian Enforcer in the Morellian Commonwealth during the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] until he retired and became a scout in the [[Republic Survey Corps/Legends|Republic Survey Corps]]. By his late 110s, Warren purchased a small planet known as [[Thrantin|OM813]], located in the [[Ollonir Boundaries]]. Over several decades, he [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] the world into multiple terrains and opened the [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]]. Warren grew wealthy as the school's dean, chancellor, and primary teacher.<ref name="AIR" />

In [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]], Warren had a disagreement with a [[Samuac]] student named [[Sisquoc]] that turned into a vendetta against not only Sisquoc but the rest of the graduating cohort, known as the [[Twilight Class]]. When Sisquoc and seven other members of the class joined the Rebel Alliance, Warren turned his hatred for the students into a generalized disregard for the Alliance as a whole. In 2 ABY, the Task Force on Alliance Security filed a report on the Morellian, whom they considered a threat to the Alliance's eight Twilight Class alumni.<ref name="AIR" />

Warren [[Parent/Legends|fathered]] at least four children through couplings with [[Barosa Warren's partner 1|two]] [[Barosa Warren's partner 2|women]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscanti]] heritage. These Morellian–Human hybrids included three sons and a daughter. Of the children, the sons [[Tole Warren]] and [[Alton Lochner]]—born Tiris Warren—served the Rebel Alliance at [[Suolriep sector/Legends|Suolriep sector]] headquarters, where both men attained the rank of [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] by 2 ABY. Lochner headed the [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Special Forces]] unit known as [[Eclipse Team]] as its [[Cloak Leader]] and was well known for rescuing troubled operatives in the field. Tole Warren, meanwhile, served aboard the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] ''[[Silent Water]]'', an assignment during which he tracked the [[Tiss'shar]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Kal-tan-shi]] through the [[Veron Minor system/Legends|Veron Minor system]]. He survived the [[Charon/Legends|Charon]] [[Battle of Stronghold|invasion]] of the planet [[Stronghold (planet)|Stronghold]] before his reassignment to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Vanden Willard/Legends|Vanden Willard's]] command at [[Delta Base]], where he decrypted Imperial communiqués. Meanwhile, Barosa Warren's son [[Roland Warren]] acted as an undercover operative in the [[Simik system/Legends|Simik system]], and his daughter [[Danlea Lochner]]—born Tara Warran—served as a Rebel [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] contact along the [[Ison Trade Corridor/Legends|Ison Corridor]].<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a sourcebook published in [[1995]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' from [[West End Games]] and written by [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[J. P. Pietrzak]], [[Trevor J. Wilson]], and [[Bill Smith]], features the Morellian character Barosa Warren as an antagonist for players to confront and describes four of Warren's half-Morellian children. Nevertheless, the book provides only cursory information about the species itself. Artist [[Mike Vilardi]] illustrated both Barosa Warren and his son, Alton Lochner.<ref name="AIR" /> Later sources mention topics related to the Morellians, such as the Morellian Weapons Conglomerate<ref name="GFTPG 19" /> and the Morellian Commonwealth,<ref name="EA 34" /> but [[2006]]'s ''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''<ref name="NEGWT xxiv-xxv" /> and [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' are the only sources to mention the species by name since their debut.<ref name="CSWE III 320">{{CSWECite|III|320|Warren, Barosa}}</ref>
